title: Committed review catches the Durable Task multi-issue combination
description: A sanitized committed-review scenario derived from a real new-service PR where the review must simultaneously catch a special-case update branch that skips concurrent field changes, a shortened new cross-resource ID field name, and redundant generic lifecycle logging, while treating duplicate retention-policy semantics as a non-blocking observation rather than a merge blocker.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models the real committed-review failure where the review only reported lifecycle logging and missed the update-path bug and the new public field naming issue.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should flag the scheduler update path because clearing one field returns early and silently skips concurrent changes to other updatable fields.
The committed review should flag the brand-new public cross-resource ID field because it uses a shortened name instead of the full `{resource_name}_id` pattern.
The committed review should flag the repeated generic lifecycle logging pattern in the new resources.
The committed review should surface the duplicate or ambiguous retention-policy semantics as a non-blocking observation when service-side precedence is not implementation-backed.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not reduce the output to helper-style churn while missing the real update-path and naming defects.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that committed review preserves multi-issue accuracy on a complex new-service PR instead of collapsing to a single easy finding.
